articles.title: Predictive Agriculture Market Set to Reach $23.5 Billion by 2028
articles.description: The global predictive agriculture market — encompassing AI-driven crop forecasting, soil analytics, and yield optimization platforms — is projected to reach $23.5 billion by 2028, growing at a CAGR of 19.3% as food security concerns and climate volatility accelerate adoption.

articles.title: John Deere's See & Spray Technology Now Covers 15 Million Acres Globally
articles.description: John Deere's computer vision-powered See & Spray system, which uses machine learning to distinguish crops from weeds and apply herbicides with surgical precision, has now been deployed across more than 15 million acres worldwide, reducing herbicide use by up to 77%.

articles.title: Startup Raises $85M to Bring Satellite-Based Crop Intelligence to Smallholder Farms
articles.description: AgriSat, a climate-tech startup using multispectral satellite imagery and AI to deliver field-level crop health and yield predictions, has closed an $85 million Series B. The platform now covers 40 million hectares across South Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa, and Latin America.

articles.title: Bayer CropScience Partners with Microsoft to Deploy AI Crop Advisory at Scale
articles.description: Bayer's agriculture division and Microsoft have announced a strategic partnership to deliver AI-driven crop advisory services to 100 million farmers by 2027, leveraging Azure AI, satellite data, and IoT soil sensors to generate personalized, field-level agronomic recommendations.

articles.title: Drought Prediction Models Now 40% More Accurate Thanks to Machine Learning
articles.description: NOAA and university researchers report that integrating machine learning with traditional climate models has improved 90-day drought forecasts by 40%. The enhanced models are being adopted by agricultural lenders and crop insurers to refine risk assessments.

articles.title: EU Approves €2.1 Billion Funding Package for Precision Farming Digitalization
articles.description: The European Union has approved a €2.1 billion investment package to accelerate precision farming adoption across member states, funding sensor networks, farm management software, drone infrastructure, and digital advisory services for 500,000 farm operations through 2027.

articles.title: Vertical Farming Giants Cut Energy Costs 30% with Predictive AI Lighting Systems
articles.description: Leading vertical farming operators AeroFarms and Bowery Farming report 30% reductions in energy costs after deploying predictive AI systems that dynamically optimize LED lighting spectra and schedules based on real-time plant growth models and energy pricing signals.

articles.title: India's Agritech Sector Attracts $1.2 Billion in VC Funding in Q1 2025
articles.description: India's agricultural technology sector recorded $1.2 billion in venture capital investment in the first quarter of 2025, with predictive crop advisory, supply chain traceability, and precision irrigation startups attracting the largest share of deals.

articles.title: Autonomous Tractors from CNH Industrial Complete First Full Planting Season Without Operators
articles.description: CNH Industrial's autonomous tractor fleet completed an entire planting and harvesting season on Iowa farms without human operators in the cab. Fleet managers supervised remotely via tablet, with AI handling obstacle avoidance, row tracking, and equipment diagnostics.

articles.title: Carbon Credit Markets Boost Precision Ag ROI as Soil Sensors Verify Sequestration
articles.description: Farmers using IoT soil sensors and precision tillage practices are monetizing carbon sequestration through voluntary carbon markets, generating $18–$45 per acre in additional annual income. Soil carbon verification powered by IoT data is emerging as a major precision ag value driver.

articles.title: Water-Smart Irrigation Startup Closes $60M Series C to Expand AI-Driven Scheduling
articles.description: CropX, an AI-powered soil monitoring and irrigation scheduling platform, raised $60 million in a Series C round to expand into new geographies and integrate satellite evapotranspiration data for hyper-local water recommendations that reduce consumption by up to 25%.

articles.title: AI-Powered Pest Forecasting Reduces Insecticide Use by 50% on California Farms
articles.description: A three-year study of California almond and tomato farms using AI-based pest pressure forecasting found that growers who followed model-driven spray recommendations reduced insecticide applications by 50% while maintaining equivalent yield and quality outcomes.

articles.title: Satellite Imagery and LLMs Combine to Auto-Generate Crop Insurance Claims
articles.description: InsureAgri, a climate fintech startup, has launched a platform that uses satellite damage imagery and large language models to automatically document and submit crop insurance claims within 48 hours of a weather event, dramatically accelerating farmer payouts.

articles.title: Nutrient Prescription Platforms Drive 15% Yield Gains in Corn Belt Trial
articles.description: A 500-field randomized trial in the US Corn Belt found that AI-generated variable-rate nutrient prescriptions, calibrated with historical yield maps and in-season NDVI data, produced an average 15% yield increase over uniform-rate fertilizer applications at comparable input cost.

articles.title: Food Giants Nestlé and Danone Join Blockchain Traceability Platform Backed by Agritech AI
articles.description: Nestlé and Danone have joined an AI-powered blockchain traceability network that links farm IoT sensor data with processing, logistics, and retail, enabling consumers to verify sustainability claims and giving brands full supply chain visibility for ESG reporting.
